sections.title: Overview
sections.content: Influenza-like illness describes an acute febrile respiratory syndrome commonly caused by influenza, COVID-19, RSV, or other respiratory viruses. Prehospital care should focus on infection control, hydration, symptom relief, and recognition of complications such as pneumonia, sepsis, myocarditis, or respiratory failure.

sections.title: Clinical Presentation
sections.content:
- Fever, chills, myalgia, headache, fatigue, or malaise
- Cough, sore throat, congestion, or coryza
- Nausea, vomiting, or diarrhoea in some patients
- Dyspnoea, chest pain, confusion, or persistent vomiting in more severe illness

sections.title: Considerations
sections.content:
- Use appropriate PPE and consider local infection-control requirements
- Do not exclude influenza or COVID-19 simply because vaccination has occurred
- Severe symptoms such as hypoxia, tachypnoea, haemodynamic instability, altered mental status, chest pain, or dehydration warrant escalation
- Antibiotics do not treat uncomplicated viral illness

sections.title: Management – Primary
sections.content:
- Apply appropriate infection-control precautions
- Primary Survey and Vital Sign Survey
- Encourage oral fluids if safe and tolerated
- Consider simple analgesia or antipyretic treatment for fever, myalgia, or headache
- Provide clear safety-net advice and local return-to-work or isolation advice where relevant

sections.title: Management – Advanced
sections.content:
- Consider ECG if chest pain, syncope, or myocarditis or ACS is a concern
- Consider IV fluids for clinically important dehydration
- Consider antiemetic treatment for troublesome vomiting
- Antivirals or doctor-directed antimicrobials may be appropriate in selected high-risk or severe cases according to local protocol and timing of illness

sections.title: Additional Information
sections.content:
- Viral cough may persist after the acute illness has improved
- Community outbreaks or high-risk workplace settings may require local reporting or additional infection-control measures
